message:
test(obligation): add E2E obligation leak stress test with rayon (bd-2ktrc.7)

Runs 100K+ random schedules in parallel verifying zero obligation leaks.
Each schedule spawns 1-8 tasks with 0-4 random obligations, resolves via
random commit/abort paths in shuffled order, with 10% chaos injection and
15% task cancellation. Configurable to 10M schedules for nightly CI via
OBLIGATION_STRESS_SCHEDULES env var. Includes binomial 95% CI validation
(target <1e-7 at 10M).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1hu19","title":"[WEDGE] FrankenLab Standalone Testing Harness","description":"Ship FrankenLab as a runtime-agnostic deterministic testing harness that provides value even without Asupersync. This is the standalone wedge deliverable — it works with any async Rust project, not just Asupersync. Features: record/replay for async code, fault injection scenarios (YAML), deterministic scheduling, virtual time. This addresses the adoption path: developers try FrankenLab for testing, then discover Asupersync for production. Must integrate with franken_trace format for cross-project replay.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:20:32.452754453Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:23:19.630188220Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenlab","testing","wedge"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19","depends_on_id":"bd-2ktrc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1hu19.1","title":"[WEDGE] Design FrankenLab scenario YAML format","description":"Design the YAML format for FrankenLab test scenarios. A scenario file defines: (1) Participants -- actors/tasks with initial state, (2) Fault schedule -- timed fault injection events (partition, crash, clock skew, message loss), (3) Assertions -- conditions to check at specific points or at quiescence, (4) Seed -- PRNG seed for deterministic scheduling, (5) Virtual time configuration -- time scale factor, max duration. The format must be: (a) Human-readable for manual authoring, (b) Machine-generatable for fuzzing, (c) Composable -- include sub-scenarios. Deliverable: JSON Schema specification, 10+ example scenarios, validation tool. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: (Design deliverable -- define the tracing contract for scenario execution.) Span: scenario::load with scenario_file, participant_count, fault_count, assertion_count. Span: scenario::execute with scenario_name, seed, strategy. DEBUG: scenario parsed: participants={n}, faults={n}, assertions={n}, virtual_time_max={max_ms}. INFO: scenario loaded: name={name}, schema_version={version}, seed={seed}. WARN: scenario validation warning: {field} has unusual value {value} -- possible authoring error. ERROR: scenario validation failed: {field} {error_description}. ERROR: scenario file not found or unparseable: path={path}, error={err}. Metrics: scenario_load_latency_us (histogram), scenario_validation_warnings_total (counter), scenario_validation_errors_total (counter).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:20:44.210835112Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T00:21:12.563957618Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T00:21:12.563826785Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","frankenlab","wedge","yaml"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:12Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1hu19.2","title":"[WEDGE] Implement deterministic scheduler for replay","description":"Implement a deterministic scheduler for FrankenLab that provides exactly reproducible execution of async tasks. The scheduler must: (1) Use a seeded PRNG for all nondeterministic scheduling decisions, (2) Record all scheduling choices to a trace file (franken_trace format), (3) Replay from a trace file with identical decisions, (4) Support exploration strategies: random, round-robin, DPOR, PCT. Replaces tokio runtime for testing via #[frankenlab::test(seed=42)]. Performance: <10% overhead for small tests, up to 5x for large tests with full recording. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: Span: scheduler::step with step_index, strategy, seed, runnable_count. Span: scheduler::record with trace_file, total_steps. Span: scheduler::replay with trace_file, expected_steps. DEBUG: scheduling decision: step={idx}, chose_task={task_id}, runnable=[{task_ids}], strategy={strategy}. DEBUG: trace event recorded: step={idx}, event_type={type}, task_id={task_id}. INFO: scheduler initialized: seed={seed}, strategy={strategy}, task_count={n}. INFO: recording started: trace_file={path}. INFO: replay started: trace_file={path}, expected_steps={n}. INFO: execution completed: total_steps={n}, wall_time_ms={ms}, virtual_time_ms={vms}. WARN: replay divergence detected: step={idx}, expected_task={expected}, actual_runnable=[{tasks}]. ERROR: replay failed: step={idx}, reason={reason}. Metrics: scheduler_step_total (counter by strategy), scheduler_step_latency_us (histogram), scheduler_runnable_count (histogram), scheduler_replay_divergence_total (counter), trace_file_size_bytes (gauge).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:20:52.710624316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T01:00:40.813021931Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T01:00:40.812886759Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["deterministic","frankenlab","scheduler","wedge"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:37Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1hu19.3","title":"[WEDGE] Implement virtual time for testing","description":"Implement virtual time system for FrankenLab enabling programmatic time control. Features: (1) Instant timeout testing -- advance time by 30s without waiting, (2) Deterministic timing -- all time-dependent ops use virtual clock, (3) Clock manipulation -- pause, resume, set, advance by delta. Implementation: (a) VirtualClock struct with atomic current_time, (b) Hook into tokio::time::sleep for virtual clock, (c) Auto-advance when all tasks blocked on sleep, (d) Manual advance via clock.advance(Duration), (e) Integration with fault injection for clock skew. Must handle ordered wake-ups, timer cancellation, I/O timeouts. Acceptance: 24-hour wall-clock test completes in <1 second with virtual time. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: Span: virtual_clock::advance with delta_ms, new_time_ms, wakeups_triggered. DEBUG: virtual clock advanced: delta_ms={delta}, new_time_ms={new_time}, wakeup_count={n}. DEBUG: virtual clock auto-advance: reason=all_tasks_blocked, next_wakeup_ms={next}. INFO: virtual clock initialized: start_time_ms={start}. INFO: virtual clock paused/resumed at time_ms={time}. WARN: virtual clock jump detected: old_time_ms={old}, new_time_ms={new}, jump_ms={jump} -- may affect lease/timeout correctness. ERROR: virtual clock attempt to go backward: current_ms={current}, requested_ms={requested}. Metrics: virtual_clock_advances_total (counter), virtual_clock_auto_advances_total (counter), virtual_clock_wakeups_triggered_total (counter), virtual_clock_current_ms (gauge), virtual_clock_wall_to_virtual_ratio (gauge).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:01.702297094Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:23.530587422Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenlab","virtual-time","wedge"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:53Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:53Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1hu19.4","title":"[WEDGE] FrankenLab CLI: frankenlab run scenario.yaml","description":"Build the FrankenLab CLI tool that runs test scenarios from YAML files. Command: frankenlab run scenario.yaml. Behavior: (1) Parse and validate scenario YAML against JSON Schema, (2) Set up participants with initial state, (3) Initialize deterministic scheduler with seed, (4) Execute scenario with fault injection per schedule, (5) Check assertions, (6) Output results as JSON or human-readable. Additional commands: validate, replay, minimize, compare. Exit codes: 0=pass, 1=assertion failure, 2=scenario error. Must produce clear, actionable error messages. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: Span: cli::run with scenario_file, seed, command. Span: cli::validate with scenario_file. Span: cli::replay with trace_file. Span: cli::minimize with scenario_file, strategy. DEBUG: CLI argument parsing: command={cmd}, args={args_json}. INFO: frankenlab {command} started: scenario={file}, seed={seed}. INFO: frankenlab {command} completed: exit_code={code}, duration_ms={ms}, assertions_passed={p}, assertions_failed={f}. WARN: scenario completed with warnings: warning_count={n}, warnings=[{warning_msgs}]. ERROR: scenario execution failed: error={err}, at_step={step}, virtual_time_ms={vt}. ERROR: CLI usage error: {message} (must include suggestion for fix). Metrics: cli_execution_total (counter by command and exit_code), cli_execution_duration_ms (histogram by command), cli_assertions_total (counter by result=pass|fail).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:09.781021657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:35.059626593Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","frankenlab","wedge"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1hu19.5","title":"[WEDGE] Unit and E2E tests for FrankenLab runtime and CLI","description":"Comprehensive test suite for the FrankenLab standalone testing harness, covering the deterministic scheduler, virtual time, scenario YAML format, and CLI.

**Unit tests:**
(1) Deterministic scheduler reproducibility — run the same program with the same seed 100 times; verify bit-identical scheduling decisions each time.
(2) PRNG scheduling strategies — verify that random, round-robin, DPOR, and PCT strategies each produce valid (non-deadlocking) schedules for a standard test program.
(3) Virtual time correctness — verify that sleep(Duration::from_secs(N)) with virtual time completes instantly in wall-clock time but advances virtual clock by exactly N seconds.
(4) Auto-advance semantics — when all tasks block on sleep, virtual time advances to the minimum next wake-up point (not further).
(5) YAML scenario parser — roundtrip property: parse(serialize(scenario)) == scenario for randomly generated scenarios (proptest).
(6) JSON Schema validation — verify that all example scenarios pass schema validation and that intentionally invalid scenarios are rejected with clear errors.
(7) Fault injection determinism — the same fault schedule with the same seed produces identical fault timing across runs.

**E2E tests:**
(8) Full scenario execution — run all 10+ example scenarios through the CLI and verify all assertions pass.
(9) Record/replay round-trip — record an execution trace, replay it, and verify bit-identical outcomes.
(10) Cross-machine reproducibility — generate artifact hashes on CI and verify they match expected golden values (proves determinism is not hardware-dependent).
(11) CLI error handling — verify that malformed YAML, missing files, and invalid seeds produce clear, actionable error messages with non-zero exit codes.
(12) Performance regression — FrankenLab overhead vs direct execution must stay below 10% for small tests (criterion.rs benchmark).

**Logging requirements:**
- Each test must log: scenario_name, seed, strategy, virtual_time_final, wall_time_ms, assertion_results, fault_count.
- On replay divergence, log the first diverging scheduling decision with full context.
- All logs in structured JSON to EvidenceLedger.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- 100% deterministic replay across 1000 runs.
- All example scenarios pass.
- Overhead below 10% for small tests, below 5x for large tests.
- All CLI error cases produce exit code != 0 with human-readable message.

**Rust implementation notes:**
- Use proptest for YAML schema fuzzing.
- Use assert_cmd + predicates for CLI integration tests.
- Use tempfile for isolated test directories.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:32.345542617Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:22:38.411148668Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenlab","testing","wedge"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:14Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:14Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:14Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:14Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:14Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1hvy1","title":"[SPORK][Registry][Core] GenServer Named Start Helper","description":"Provide helper that combines:
- acquire NameLease
- start GenServer under supervisor
- release lease on stop","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance:
- Behavior is cancel-correct and deterministic.
- Lab tests exist or are explicitly referenced.
- No stale names after stop/crash.","notes":"Implemented supervised named-start helper in src/gen_server.rs: added NamedGenServerStart + named_gen_server_start() (ChildStart adapter) that acquires NameLease via spawn_named_gen_server, stores GenServer task, and registers deterministic sync finalizer to cleanup_task(task_id) + resolve lease on region stop. Added regression tests named_start_helper_supervisor_stop_cleans_registry and named_start_helper_crash_then_stop_cleans_registry. Re-exported helper in src/spork.rs (spork::gen_server + prelude). Added SpawnError::NameRegistrationFailed in src/runtime/state.rs for deterministic name-collision surfacing in supervised start.\n\nValidation results:\n- cargo fmt --check: PASS (after cargo fmt)\n- cargo check --all-targets: initially PASS after implementation, later BLOCKED by unrelated shared-tree syntax error in src/cx/scope.rs (unexpected closing delimiter around line 1980).\n- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings: BLOCKED by unrelated shared-tree breakages in src/combinator/circuit_breaker.rs and later src/cx/scope.rs.\n- cargo test: runs deep into suite, our unit tests pass, but full run fails on unrelated tests/distributed_spawn.rs and later shared-tree compile issues.\n","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilentGlen","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:23:46.541881700Z","created_by":"FuchsiaRiver","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:43:57.343549231Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:43:57.343478690Z","close_reason":"Implemented named start helper + tests; local full gates currently blocked by unrelated shared-tree failures in other files","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["genserver","registry","spork"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hvy1","depends_on_id":"bd-133q8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:49Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hvy1","depends_on_id":"bd-23az1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:49Z","created_by":"import"}]}
@@ -2211,7 +2211,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2ktrc.4","title":"[TESTING] Implement fault injection for clock skew","description":"Implement fault injection for simulating clock skew between Asupersync actors. Clock skew tests whether timeout-based protocols (lease expiry, budget deadlines, heartbeat detection) remain correct when clocks diverge.

BACKGROUND: In distributed systems, clock skew is a fundamental source of bugs. Even with NTP, clocks can diverge by milliseconds (normal) to seconds (pathological). The CALM theorem (Graveyard section 13.3) notes that monotone programs are clock-insensitive, but threshold-based operations (lease expiry, timeout detection) are inherently clock-dependent. Testing with simulated clock skew validates that the system uses causal ordering (logical clocks) rather than wall-clock assumptions for correctness-critical decisions.

RUST IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE: (1) Define ClockSkewInjector struct with per_actor_offset: HashMap<ActorId, Duration> for static skew and drift_rate: HashMap<ActorId, f64> for progressive drift (ms/s). (2) Hook into the virtual time system from bd-1hu19.3: each actors VirtualClock applies its offset before returning the current time. (3) Clock jump: schedule a sudden offset change at a specific virtual time point to simulate NTP correction. (4) Monotonicity violation: temporarily return a time earlier than the previous read (detects code that assumes monotonic clocks). (5) Integration with FrankenLab scenario YAML: clock_skew section in fault schedule specifying per-actor offsets, drift rates, and jump events. (6) Use quanta crate for high-resolution clock abstraction that can be swapped for virtual clock in tests.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) Lease expiry is correct despite clock skew -- use causal ordering, not wall-clock, for lease validity determination. Specifically: a lease acquired at logical time T with duration D expires at logical time T+D regardless of wall-clock skew. (2) Budget deadlines account for worst-case skew -- budget remaining calculation uses the maximum clock reading across all participants. (3) Heartbeat detection uses logical clocks -- a heartbeat received with a different wall-clock timestamp is accepted if the logical clock is within bounds. (4) No liveness violation -- system makes progress despite bounded clock skew (verified: all sagas complete within 2x the non-skewed baseline time). (5) Clock jump does not cause spurious lease expiry or budget exhaustion. (6) Monotonicity violation is detected and logged as a warning (system continues, does not crash).

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Each clock skew injection emits structured log: actor_id, original_time_ms, skewed_time_ms, offset_ms, drift_rate, event_type (static_offset|drift|jump|monotonicity_violation). All injected faults logged to EvidenceLedger: component=clock_skew_injector, action=skew_applied, top_features includes max_skew_ms, affected_actor_count. Aggregate stats at test completion: max_observed_skew_ms, monotonicity_violations_count, lease_expiry_correctness (pass/fail count).

TESTING APPROACH: (1) Unit tests: verify ClockSkewInjector correctly applies offsets and drift rates. (2) Lease tests: acquire lease under various skew conditions (0ms, 10ms, 100ms, 1s), verify correct expiry. (3) Budget tests: propagate budget through actors with different clock offsets, verify deadline correctness. (4) Heartbeat tests: verify heartbeat detection with skewed clocks. (5) Property tests (proptest): for any bounded clock skew (< 1s), all protocols eventually complete. (6) Integration: run full saga under clock skew via FrankenLab scenario, verify identical outcome to non-skewed execution.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:19:59.965023589Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T22:07:34.683055179Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T22:07:22.798011739Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clock-skew","fault-injection","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2ktrc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:43Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":1409,"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented clock skew fault injection. SkewClock wraps TimeSource with static offset, drift, one-time jump, and jitter. Integrates with TimerDriver. 18 unit + 15 integration tests, clippy clean, fmt clean. Commit 59eec7f.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:07:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ktrc.5","title":"[TESTING] Loom-based concurrency tests for scheduler queues","description":"Implement Loom-based concurrency tests for Asupersync's scheduler queues and lock-free data structures. Loom exhaustively explores all possible thread interleavings to find concurrency bugs that are nearly impossible to catch with random testing.

BACKGROUND (Tier C Formal Assurance, Graveyard section 0.14): Loom provides model-checked concurrency testing by controlling thread scheduling and exploring all valid interleavings. Unlike stress tests that probabilistically find bugs, loom guarantees that if a bug exists within the exploration bound, it will be found. This corresponds to Tier C (Deductive Verification) on the formal assurance ladder because it provides exhaustive coverage within bounds.

RUST IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE: (1) Add loom as a dev-dependency behind a cfg(loom) feature flag. (2) Replace std::sync::atomic with loom::sync::atomic in testable code using conditional imports: #[cfg(loom)] use loom::sync::atomic::AtomicUsize; #[cfg(not(loom))] use std::sync::atomic::AtomicUsize. (3) Create a separate test binary: tests/loom_tests.rs that only runs under cargo test --features loom. (4) Use loom::model(|| { ... }) to wrap each test case. (5) For work-stealing deque: test push/pop from owner thread, steal from multiple thief threads, verify no lost items and no duplicates. (6) For MPSC channel: test concurrent sends from N producers, single consumer recv, verify total ordering within each producer and no message loss. (7) For task state machine: test concurrent wake/cancel/complete transitions, verify exactly one terminal state is reached. (8) For budget counter: test concurrent decrements from N threads, verify counter never goes below zero and final value equals initial minus total decrements.

COVERAGE TARGETS: (1) Work-stealing deque: all push/pop/steal interleavings with 2 threads, 3 operations each. (2) MPSC channel: all send/recv interleavings with 3 senders, 1 receiver, 2 messages each. (3) Task state machine: all wake/cancel/complete interleavings with 2 threads. (4) Budget counter: all decrement/check interleavings with 3 threads, 2 decrements each.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) All loom tests pass with max_preemptions=2 (CI fast mode). (2) All loom tests pass with max_preemptions=4 (nightly thorough mode). (3) No data races detected by loom (loom catches these automatically via its memory model). (4) No deadlocks (all model executions terminate within bounded steps). (5) Correct final state in all explored interleavings. (6) Loom tests complete in < 5 minutes for CI, < 30 minutes for nightly.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Each loom test logs: test_name, max_preemptions, total_interleavings_explored, execution_time_ms, pass_fail. On failure: log the specific interleaving that caused the failure (loom provides a trace of scheduling decisions). Summary at test suite completion: total_tests, total_interleavings, unique_bugs_found. All output in structured JSON for CI parsing.

TESTING APPROACH (meta): (1) Start with the simplest data structure (budget counter) to validate the loom test infrastructure. (2) Progressively add more complex structures. (3) Use loom::thread::yield_now() at critical points to increase exploration coverage. (4) Compare loom results against tokio-test stress tests to validate that loom catches bugs stress tests miss.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:20:09.545514942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T22:40:48.342030150Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T22:40:48.341895630Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","loom","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2ktrc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:15Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ktrc.6","title":"[TESTING] E2E test: deterministic replay parity across 1000 random schedules","description":"End-to-end test that validates Asupersync's deterministic replay guarantee across 1000 random schedules using the Lab runtime.

BACKGROUND: Deterministic replay is Asupersync's core value proposition -- the ability to record a concurrent execution and replay it identically on any machine. This test validates that promise at scale. The FrankenLab deterministic scheduler (bd-1hu19.2) controls all nondeterministic choices (thread scheduling, timer resolution, I/O ordering) and records them to a trace file. Replay uses the same trace to make identical choices.

RUST IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE: (1) Use FrankenLab's deterministic scheduler with random seed generation: let seed = rand::thread_rng().gen::<u64>(); (2) Test program: a realistic workload with multiple concurrent tasks, channels, obligations, cancellation, and budget propagation. At minimum: 10 tasks, 5 channels, 3 obligation types, 1 cancellation event, budget propagation through 3 levels. (3) For each of 1000 iterations: (a) Generate random seed, (b) Run workload under Lab scheduler with recording, (c) Capture output hash (SHA-256 of all observable side effects: task return values, channel message order, obligation lifecycle events), (d) Replay from recorded trace, (e) Capture replay output hash, (f) Assert record_hash == replay_hash. (4) Use rayon::par_iter for parallel execution of the 1000 iterations. (5) Store all seeds and hashes in a results CSV for analysis.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) 100% replay determinism: all 1000 iterations produce identical record/replay hashes. (2) Test completes in < 10 minutes on CI (parallelized). (3) Any replay divergence produces a detailed diff showing the first diverging event with full context. (4) Test is itself deterministic: running the test with the same meta-seed produces the same 1000 sub-seeds. (5) Cross-platform: hashes are identical on x86_64 and aarch64 (floating-point determinism verified).

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Per-iteration structured JSON log: iteration_index, seed, record_hash, replay_hash, match (bool), record_time_ms, replay_time_ms, event_count. Summary log at completion: total_iterations, match_count, mismatch_count, determinism_rate (target 1.0), total_wall_time_ms, avg_replay_overhead_percent. On divergence: full event-level diff with timestamps, task IDs, and event types for both record and replay traces. All logs emitted to both stdout (CI visible) and JSONL file for offline analysis.

TESTING APPROACH: (1) Smoke test: single iteration with known seed, verify manually computed expected hash. (2) Regression: if any iteration ever fails, add its seed to a pinned regression set that runs on every CI build. (3) Statistical: track determinism rate over time in a dashboard -- any rate below 1.0 is a P0 bug.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TealFinch","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:20:16.147531077Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T00:50:50.429489338Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["deterministic-replay","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2if.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2ktrc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:13Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":1413,"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"[2026-02-14 TealFinch] Started and implemented deterministic replay parity sweep in tests/replay_e2e_suite.rs. Added: (1) seed-sweep harness test deterministic_replay_parity_seed_sweep_1000, (2) richer parity workload with mixed tasks + cancellation, (3) deterministic meta-seed/iteration env controls, (4) structured JSON/CSV artifact emission (target/replay in CI or env override), (5) SHA-256 record/replay hash comparison + mismatch diagnostics.\n\nValidation run:\n- cargo fmt --check ✅\n- cargo check --all-targets ✅ (shared-tree warning in src/lab/scenario_runner.rs)\n- cargo test --test replay_e2e_suite deterministic_replay_parity_seed_sweep_1000 -- --nocapture ✅\n  result: 1000/1000 matched, determinism_ppm=1_000_000, total_wall_time_ms≈4400.\n- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings ❌ blocked by pre-existing unrelated clippy violations in src/lab/scenario_runner.rs and src/obligation/saga.rs in current shared tree.\n\nKeeping bead in_progress pending broader acceptance hardening (cross-platform hash parity, additional workload dimensions, CI profile integration).","created_at":"2026-02-14T00:50:50Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2ktrc.7","title":"[TESTING] E2E test: no obligation leaks under 10M random schedules","description":"End-to-end stress test that runs 10 million random schedules with the Lab runtime and verifies zero obligation leaks. For each schedule: (1) Spawn a random DAG of tasks with random obligations (SendPermit, Lease, Reserve), (2) Apply random faults (cancel, timeout, panic), (3) After quiescence, verify all obligations have been released (obligation counter == 0), (4) Log any leaks with full schedule trace for reproduction. Configuration: Use rayon for parallel execution across cores. Target: complete 10M schedules in <30 minutes on CI. Statistics: Track leak rate with exact binomial confidence interval — target is 0 leaks with 95% CI upper bound <1e-7. This is the ultimate obligation safety validation — if we can survive 10M random schedules without a leak, the system is robust.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:20:25.150268482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:23:26.303864660Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","obligation-leaks","stress","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2if.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:29Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2ktrc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:29Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2l8s","title":"[AA][Trace] Causality‑preserving trace compression","description":"## Goal
Add trace compression via lattice homomorphisms that preserve causal correctness.

## Why
We need low‑overhead traces without losing replay or proof validity.

## Deliverables
- Compression scheme with formal preservation proof sketch.
- Encoder/decoder implementation.
- Benchmarks for size and CPU overhead.

## Acceptance
- Replay correctness preserved after compress/decompress.
- Compression ratio meets target without hot‑path regressions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:10:02.946489489Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:48:07.348939803Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T03:48:07.348866778Z","close_reason":"Added src/trace/compression.rs: Causality-preserving trace compression with 3 levels (Lossless, Structural, Skeleton). Structural removes noise events (wakes, user traces, timer events). Skeleton keeps only lifecycle + obligation + cancel + region events. CompressedTrace with ratio/events_removed metrics and embedded TraceCertificate. validate_compressed() for integrity checking. 8 tests. Exported in trace/mod.rs.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2l8s","depends_on_id":"bd-39gs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:50Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2l8s","depends_on_id":"bd-s0zq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:50Z","created_by":"import"}]}
